title: Frontend User Interface
summary: Build the React frontend with timer display, controls, mode selector, and task input according to the design specification.
businessValue: As a user, I want a clean, minimal interface so that I can focus on my Pomodoro workflow without distraction.

stories.title: Page Layout and Header
stories.userValue: As a user, I want a clean header and centered page layout so that the app is visually organized and the timer card is the main focus.

acceptanceCriteria.then: The header displays 'Pomodoro' as the app title with 28px, 700 weight font

acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is displayed on different screen sizes
acceptanceCriteria.then: The main content is centered horizontally with a maximum width of 900px
No reports, settings, menu buttons, or sign-in links are visible

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Page background uses deep navy color
acceptanceCriteria.then: The app background is #0F172A

definitionOfDone: Header component created with 'Pomodoro' title and correct typography
Page uses 900px max-width centered container
Dark mode background color (#0F172A) applied to page
Responsive layout maintains centering on mobile and tablet
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Timer Card Container and Structure
stories.userValue: As a user, I want the timer displayed in a prominent card so that I can easily see the countdown and interact with timer controls.

acceptanceCriteria.then: A card component is displayed with background #1E293B, 1px solid #334155 border, and 20px border radius
The card has 40px padding and a width of approximately 720px max, centered on the page

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Card contains all timer elements in correct order
acceptanceCriteria.then: The card structure includes: mode selector at top, timer display in center, and control buttons below

definitionOfDone: TimerCard component created with specified styling
Background, border, border-radius, and padding match design spec
Component is responsive and centered
All child elements are correctly positioned
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Timer Display
stories.userValue: As a user, I want to see a large, clear timer display so that I can easily track how much time remains in my Pomodoro session.

acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is loaded and Focus mode is selected
acceptanceCriteria.then: The timer display shows '25:00' in 112px, 700 weight font with -2px letter spacing

acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is loaded and Break mode is selected
acceptanceCriteria.then: The timer display shows '05:00' in 112px, 700 weight font

acceptanceCriteria.given: The timer is running and set to 25:00
acceptanceCriteria.then: The timer display updates to show '24:50' and continues counting down
The display always shows two digits for minutes and two digits for seconds (e.g., 02:15)
The timer text color is #F8FAFC (near white)

definitionOfDone: TimerDisplay component created with 112px font size and 700 weight
Default values 25:00 and 05:00 display correctly for respective modes
MM:SS format is correctly implemented
Real-time countdown updates correctly
Text color and letter spacing match design spec
Timer stops at 00:00 and does not go negative
Unit tests verify countdown logic
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Focus and Break Mode Selector
stories.userValue: As a user, I want to switch between Focus and Break modes so that I can easily alternate between 25-minute work sessions and 5-minute breaks.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Mode selector shows Focus and Break options
acceptanceCriteria.then: Two clickable mode buttons labeled 'Focus' and 'Break' are visible with 18px, 600 weight font

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Focus mode is active by default
acceptanceCriteria.then: The Focus button has active styling: background #0891B2, text #F8FAFC, 10px border radius
The Focus button shows active styling and Break button shows inactive styling (transparent background, #CBD5E1 text)

acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is in Focus mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: Break mode becomes active and timer resets to 05:00

acceptanceCriteria.given: An inactive mode button is displayed
acceptanceCriteria.then: The button background changes to #334155 and text becomes #F8FAFC

definitionOfDone: ModeSelector component created with Focus and Break buttons
Active mode styling correctly applied (background #0891B2)
Inactive mode styling correctly applied (transparent, #CBD5E1 text)
Hover states work as specified
Clicking a mode updates the active mode state
Mode switching resets the timer to appropriate duration
Running timer stops when mode is switched
Unit tests verify mode switching behavior
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Timer Control Buttons - Start/Pause and Reset
stories.userValue: As a user, I want Start, Pause, and Reset buttons so that I can control the timer independently.

acceptanceCriteria.then: A button labeled 'START' is displayed with background #22D3EE, 24px 700 weight font, 12px border radius, approximately 320px width and 72px height

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Start button changes to Pause on timer start
acceptanceCriteria.given: The timer is not running and Start button is visible
acceptanceCriteria.then: The button label changes to 'PAUSE' and timer begins counting down

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Pause button changes to Start on pause
acceptanceCriteria.given: The timer is running with Pause button visible
acceptanceCriteria.then: The button label changes to 'START' and timer stops at current remaining time
A secondary Reset button is visible below or beside the Start/Pause button
The button has transparent background, 1px solid #475569 border, #CBD5E1 text, and 12px border radius

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Reset button returns timer to default
acceptanceCriteria.given: The timer is at any state and showing a different time than default
acceptanceCriteria.then: Timer stops and returns to the selected mode's default duration (25:00 or 05:00)

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Reset button hover state is correct
acceptanceCriteria.then: Background changes to #334155 and text becomes #F8FAFC

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Start button hover state is correct
acceptanceCriteria.then: Background changes to #67E8F9

definitionOfDone: TimerControls component created with Start/Pause and Reset buttons
Start button styling matches spec (#22D3EE background, 24px, 700 weight, 320px x 72px)
Pause button styling matches Start button styling
Reset button styling matches spec (transparent, #475569 border, #CBD5E1 text)
Button state toggling (Start ↔ Pause) works correctly
Reset button resets timer to default duration for current mode
Hover states work for both buttons
Buttons are disabled when appropriate (e.g., during specific transitions if needed)
Unit tests verify button state transitions and timer control logic
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Session Message Display
stories.userValue: As a user, I want to see contextual messages so that I'm reminded of the current mode's purpose.

acceptanceCriteria.then: The message 'Time to focus.' appears below the timer with 20px font, #CBD5E1 text color, center aligned
The message 'Take a short break.' appears below the timer with 20px font, #CBD5E1 text color, center aligned

acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is in Focus mode showing 'Time to focus.'
acceptanceCriteria.then: The message immediately changes to 'Take a short break.'

definitionOfDone: SessionMessage component created with correct text for each mode
Focus mode displays 'Time to focus.'
Break mode displays 'Take a short break.'
Text styling matches spec (20px, #CBD5E1, center aligned)
Message updates when mode changes
Message is positioned below timer card
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Task Input Section
stories.userValue: As a user, I want to enter my key task so that I can stay focused on my main objective during the Pomodoro session.

acceptanceCriteria.then: Below the timer area, a section labeled 'Key Task' is displayed with 24px, 700 weight font in #F8FAFC
An input field with placeholder 'What are you focusing on?' is displayed with 18px font

acceptanceCriteria.given: The task input field is visible
acceptanceCriteria.then: The input has background #1E293B, border 1px solid #475569, text #F8FAFC, placeholder #94A3B8, and 12px border radius

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Task can be entered before timer starts
acceptanceCriteria.given: The task input field is visible and timer is not running
acceptanceCriteria.when: User types a task in the input field
acceptanceCriteria.then: The task text is displayed in the input and can be edited

acceptanceCriteria.given: A task is entered and timer is started
acceptanceCriteria.then: The task remains visible and readable in the input field
The task is removed and the placeholder text is visible again

acceptanceCriteria.given: The task input field is visible
acceptanceCriteria.then: The input border changes to #22D3EE (cyan accent)

definitionOfDone: TaskInput component created with section heading 'Key Task'
Input field has correct placeholder text
Input styling matches spec (background #1E293B, border #475569, text #F8FAFC)
Focus state applies cyan border (#22D3EE)
Task text can be entered, edited, and cleared
Task persists when timer is running
Task input is accessible and works on keyboard and mouse
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Dark Mode Color Scheme and Theme
stories.userValue: As a user, I want a calm, dark theme so that the app is easy on my eyes during focused work sessions.

acceptanceCriteria.then: All UI elements use colors from the specified palette: backgrounds, text, borders, accents, and hover states
All text is readable with sufficient contrast against backgrounds (WCAG AA standards)

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Cyan accent color is used for interactive elements
acceptanceCriteria.then: Primary buttons and focus states use cyan (#22D3EE), hover uses bright cyan (#67E8F9), and active uses deep cyan (#0891B2)
Text uses the font stack: Inter, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif

definitionOfDone: Global CSS color variables or theme system is set up with all specified colors
All components use theme colors consistently
Font stack is applied to all text elements
Contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards for all text/background combinations
Dark mode colors are applied throughout the app
No hardcoded color values in components (uses theme variables)
Visual regression testing confirms colors match design spec
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged

stories.title: Responsive Layout
stories.userValue: As a user, I want the app to work well on different screen sizes so that I can use the Pomodoro timer on any device.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Layout is centered on all screen sizes
acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is viewed on desktop, tablet, and mobile
acceptanceCriteria.then: Content is centered with maximum 900px width and maintains alignment on all screen sizes

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Timer card is readable on mobile
acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is viewed on a mobile device (375px viewport)
acceptanceCriteria.then: The timer card remains readable with appropriate padding and font sizes are scaled proportionally

acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is viewed on a mobile device
acceptanceCriteria.then: Buttons are large enough to tap comfortably (minimum 44px height for touch targets)

acceptanceCriteria.given: The app is viewed on any screen size
acceptanceCriteria.then: Content fits within the viewport without requiring horizontal scrolling

definitionOfDone: Responsive design is implemented with media queries and flexible layouts
Max-width of 900px is maintained and honored
Content remains centered on all screen sizes
Touch targets are minimum 44px height on mobile
Font sizes are readable on mobile without zoom
No horizontal scrolling on any viewport width from 320px to 2560px
Tested on mobile (iPhone), tablet (iPad), and desktop viewports
No console errors or warnings
Code reviewed and merged
